title: Lettre d'intention
text_preview: LETTRE D'INTENTION ENTRE LA RÉPUBLIQUE FRANCAISE ET LA RÉPUBLIQUE DU GHANA RELATIVE AU DEVELOPPEMENT DES RELATIONS BILATÉRALES DANS LE DOMAINE DE LA DÉFENSE Le Gouvernement de la République française et le Gouvernement de la République du Ghana, cidessous dénommés « les Parties » ; Désirant agir dans un esprit d'amitié et de compréhension mutuelle ; Rappelant l'engagement de leurs Etats respectifs au profit du renfo…

pages.text:
LETTRE D'INTENTION

ENTRE

LA RÉPUBLIQUE FRANCAISE

ET

LA RÉPUBLIQUE DU GHANA

RELATIVE

AU DEVELOPPEMENT DES RELATIONS BILATÉRALES

DANS LE DOMAINE DE LA DÉFENSE

La présente lettre d'intention ne constitue ni un accord ni un engagement financier juridiquement contraignants pour les Parties, au sens du droit national ou international. Tout litige en relation avec l'interprétation ou la mise en œuvre de la présente lettre d'intention sera résolu par consultation entre les Parties.

Aucune information relative à la présente lettre d'intention ne sera diffusée sans le consentement préalable des Parties.

La présente lettre d'intention est signée à Paris le 11 juillet 2019, en deux versions rédigées en français et en anglais.

Pour le Gouvernement de la

République française

Pour le Gouvernement de la

République du Ghana

Le Ministre de l'Europe et des Affaires

étrangères

Jean-Yves LE DRIAN

Le Ministre de la Défense

Dominic NITIWUL

LETTER OF INTENT

BETWEEN

THE FRENCH REPUBLIC

AND

THE REPUBLIC OF GHANA

ON THE

DEVELOPMENT OF BILATERAL RELATIONS

IN THE FIELD OF DEFENCE

i

The Government of the French Republic and the Government of the Republic of Ghana, hereinafter referred to as "the Parties";

Willing to act in a spirit of friendship and mutual understanding;

Recalling the commitment of their respective States for the benefits of peace and security in

Africa

Affirming their will to reinforce the relevant frameworks of multilateral cooperation in the field of defence, peace and security

Willing to increase their cooperation against the spread of terrorist armed groups in western

Africa

Welcoming the reinforcement of the bilateral relations between their two States, as concretised by the annual political dialogue

Commending the quality of the 2019 Ghana -French co-chairmanship of the Group of G7++

Friends of the Gulf of Guinea,

Declare in this Letter of Intent, their intention to strengthen their cooperation in the field of security and defence by implementing the following initiatives:

One:

The assistance of France, within its capacities, to the reinforcement of the capabilities of the

Ghana Armed Forces in the field of counter-terrorism.

Two:

In the short term, the exchange of information regarding security, especially in the field of the fight against terrorist armed groups.

In the mid-term, the signing of an agreement on confidential information in the field of defence and security.

Three:

The contribution of the Parties, within their capacities, to the strengthening of the regional security and defence cooperation, through the support of France in the area of advice, training and information-sharing, and the involvement of Ghana in information-sharing and coordination of actions against terrorist armed groups.

Four:

The joint commitment to support the operationalization of the Yaoundé architecture for maritime security and safety in the Gulf of Guinea, notably through the strengthening of the

Accra multinational coordination centre.

The strengthening of the naval cooperation between France and Ghana.

Five:

The continuation by France of the French language education for the benefit of Ghanaian units deployed in peacekeeping operations and officers of the Ghana Armed Forces.

The Parties will ensure the implementation of the progress of the bilateral relations in the field of defence in the framework of the annual bilateral political dialogue.

2

This Letter of Intent does not constitute an agreement nor a financial engagement legally binding for the Parties under national or international law. Any dispute regarding the interpretation or the implementation of this Letter of Intent will be resolved by a bilateral consultation between the Parties.

No information regarding this Letter of Intent shall be disclosed without prior consent of the

Parties.

English languages is signed in Paris on 1' July, 2019, in two versions in the French and

For the Government of the Republic of France

Mute.

The Minister for Europe and

Foreign Affairs

Jean-Yves LE DRIAN

For the Government of the Republie of Ghana

The Minister for Defence

Dominic NITIWUL
